mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signal, as indicated by a 4-month increase in HOUST, suggests an expansionary monetary policy stance that boosts consumer spending and subsequently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 4k units in consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month rise in UNRATE, suggests a weakening labor market that compresses consumer spending and subsequently drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production signal, as indicated by a 2-month decrease in INDPRO, suggests a weakening supply chain that compresses consumer spending and subsequently drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales signal, as indicated by a 1-month increase in RSAFS, suggests an expansionary monetary policy stance that boosts consumer spending and subsequently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the monetary policy rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month decrease in FEDFUNDS, suggests an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and subsequently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production signal, as indicated by a 2-month decrease in INDPRO, suggests a weakening supply chain that compresses consumer spending and subsequently drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
